task: Launch-quality advanced task: Exam Blueprint Coverage Optimization. Reality context: an exam committee must assemble a form matching a new professional blueprint. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=domain_weight_pct, metric2=item_count, metric3=difficulty_mean, metric4=coverage_gap_pct; records: R1 domain_weight_pct=19.6 item_count=592 difficulty_mean=61.86 coverage_gap_pct=85.9; R2 domain_weight_pct=53.2 item_count=889 difficulty_mean=40.28 coverage_gap_pct=68.5; R3 domain_weight_pct=8.9 item_count=697 difficulty_mean=32.25 coverage_gap_pct=83.1; R4 domain_weight_pct=93.5 item_count=313 difficulty_mean=40.23 coverage_gap_pct=2.8; limit_domain_weight_pct=58.1; limit_item_count=701; limit_difficulty_mean=21.26; decision_budget_usd=227500; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="guidance required separating verified evidence from unresolved findings". Rules and constraints: Treat confidence below 0.70, missing provenance, or contradictory record status as needs_review even when the numeric score passes. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: optimize coverage and difficulty balance; convert the packet into an auditable evidence table, cite the governing values, and list unresolved assumptions. Deliver blueprint_coverage.xlsx + exam_committee_memo.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
